piece.title: Shake the Disease (FormaComposition) v5
piece.notes: Structure: Intro -> Verse -> Chorus -> Verse -> Chorus -> Bridge -> Chorus(final) -> Outro. Verse: Dm-C-F-Bb (aeolian, warm). Chorus: Dm-Fm-Db-Bb (Fm is borrowed bIIIm — the DM signature dark move). Bridge: Bb-C-Bb-Dm. Primary melody carries the 'plea' motif throughout (rhythm:motif). Two counterpoint voices ride above/below: counterpoint 1 (arpeggio character, above) and counterpoint 2 (counterline character, below), each rhythmically independent. v4: chorus is now a single shared definition in song form, repeated via exact_repeat so chorus_1 and chorus_2 are byte-identical — same seed, same rest_probability, same velocities. chorus_final remains a distinct, larger climactic variant (16 bars, fuller texture) and is NOT a repeat. v5: v4 validated and rendered but never actually exercised per-voice motif assignment (item 9 / MT-1) — neither counterpoint entry set a motif field anywhere, so both voices rendered with default rule-driven pitch and generic free-species rhythm, no motif involvement at all, despite this piece's own notes describing 'arpeggio character' and 'counterline character'. Added motif: 'arpeggio' to counterpoint 1 and motif: 'counterline' to counterpoint 2 in every section that has a counterpoint block (verse_1, chorus, verse_2, bridge, chorus_final, outro — intro has none, voices silent there per its own notes). Pitch selection is untouched by this change — consonance/voice-leading stays fully rule-driven (MT-1 decision (i)); only each voice's RHYTHM now comes from its assigned motif's tiled rhythm instead of a generic groove/density grid. This is the first version of this piece that actually renders the three-named-motif structure theme_shake_v2.json declares. v5 correction: the motif field only has an audible effect on free-species voices — first-species counterpoint is rhythmically locked to the melody by definition and silently ignores any assigned motif (verified, this is MT-1's intended design, not a bug). This piece's species assignment per voice position flips between verse and chorus sections (a deliberate v4 texture choice), so a motif fixed to a voice index was inert in some sections and active in others. Corrected: motif is now present only where that voice is actually free-species in that section — arpeggio is silent (locked to melody's plea rhythm) in chorus/chorus_final, where the above voice is first-species; counterline is silent in verse_1/verse_2, where the below voice is first-species. Both are active in bridge (both voices free). This is the first version of this piece where every motif field present is genuinely audible, none silently inert.

intro.notes: Bass pulse only. Primary melody (plea motif) enters as a fragment. Counterpoint voices silent here — sparse texture only. Harmony stays sustained, no strumming yet.

verse_1.notes: Dm-C-F-Bb feel. Primary melody carries plea motif, lyrical. Counterpoint 1 (arpeggio character) interweaves above; counterpoint 2 (counterline character) below.

chorus.notes: The darkness hits. bVI is the Db major borrowed chord (one semitone below tonic). Harmony strikes on the plea motif's syncopated rhythm — Ob-La-Di-style chord hits. This single definition is reused for both choruses via exact_repeat so the hook is identical each time.

verse_2.notes: Same feel as verse 1, motif transform applied to the primary melody. Counterpoint 1 slightly pulled back for variation.

bridge.notes: Bb-C-Bb-Dm. Tension and release. Counterpoint 1 and 2 move in contrary motion, building toward the final chorus.

chorus_final.notes: Extended final chorus — 16 bars, intentionally NOT a repeat of chorus. Primary melody and both counterpoint voices at full intensity, peak interweave.

outro.notes: Voices dissolve in reverse order. Bass pedal on D. Counterpoint 2 drops out first, then counterpoint 1. Harmony returns to sustain, mirroring the intro.
